region_of_origin: Northwest China (Shaanxi / Gansu region), served in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general rice noodle dish class; no specific Northwest Chinese flat rice noodle entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GKM covers rice noodles as a category. No specific Northwest-style flat rice noodle code exists in FoodEx2; closest category anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific prepared noodle dish. SNOMED CT coverage for regional Asian noodle dishes is limited.

preparation_method: mixed with sesame paste and vinegar dressing

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Northwest Style Flat Rice Noodles is approximately 300 g (one plate), providing around 492 kcal, 19.5 g protein, 12.6 g fat, and 75.6 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Northwest Style Flat Rice Noodles — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Northwest Style Flat Rice Noodles (cr_id G00010084). All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice noodles (flat, boiled)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database specifically for Northwest-style flat rice noodles with chili-sesame dressing. The GI of 61 is derived from the closest analogous entry: flat rice noodles (boiled), reported at GI ~61 (glucose reference) in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The chili oil, sesame paste, and vinegar dressing components are expected to modestly lower the effective GI relative to plain boiled noodles due to fat, protein, and acetic acid content; however, the base GI of 61 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 75.57 g) / 100 ≈ 46.1 / 3 ≈ 15.4 for a 300 g serving (carbohydrate 25.19 g/100 g × 300 g = 75.57 g; GL = 61 × 75.57 / 100 = 46.1; reported per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100 g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 693 mg sodium, representing 30% of the 2300 mg daily limit, partially offset by high potassium (327 mg/100 g, ~981 mg per serving) consistent with DASH diet principles.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and chili oil, choose low-sodium soy sauce where available, and limit portion size to one standard plate to keep sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 25.19 g/100 g with a medium GI of ~61; dietary fibre at 3.7 g/100 g moderates postprandial glucose response, and the vinegar and fat components of the dressing may further attenuate glycaemic excursion.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit serving size to 150–200 g, pair the dish with additional non-starchy vegetables to increase fibre, and monitor postprandial glucose given the moderate GI of flat rice noodles.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 4.21 g/100 g from chili oil and sesame paste; cholesterol is 0 mg/100 g; iron 2.55 mg/100 g and potassium 327 mg/100 g are cardioprotective; sodium at 231 mg/100 g warrants attention for CVD patients on sodium restriction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cardiovascular disease patients should moderate portion size to control sodium and calorie intake, request reduced chili oil, and benefit from the dish's zero cholesterol and high potassium content, which support vascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chili oil contains nightshade-derived capsaicin; nightshade compounds (solanine-related alkaloids) may aggravate inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA-sensitive individuals should substitute chili oil with plain sesame oil, retain the vinegar and sesame paste components for their minor anti-inflammatory benefit, and increase the vegetable proportion of the dish.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Northwest Style Flat Rice Noodles (西北风味宽米粉) is a Chinese-origin noodle dish served in Singapore, inspired by the bold flavours of China's northwest provinces such as Shaanxi and Gansu. The dish features wide, flat rice noodles — smooth, slightly chewy, and gluten-free in their base form — tossed in a pungent dressing of chili oil, sesame paste, soy sauce, and black or rice vinegar, garnished with garlic, spring onions, and fresh or blanched vegetables. The flavour profile is savoury, nutty, spicy, and tangy simultaneously. Per 100 g, the dish provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, 25.19 g carbohydrate, 3.7 g dietary fibre, 231 mg sodium, 327 mg potassium, 54 mg calcium, 40 mg magnesium, and 2.55 mg iron. The glycemic index is approximately 61 (medium), making portion control important for individuals with type 2 diabetes. The high potassium-to-sodium ratio is consistent with DASH diet principles and is relevant for hypertension management. The dish is vegetarian and vegan-compatible when prepared without animal-derived additions. Chili oil is a nightshade-derived ingredient and may be a concern for rheumatoid arthritis-sensitive individuals. The sesame paste and vinegar components contribute healthy unsaturated fats and acetic acid respectively, both of which have minor metabolic benefits. This dish is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Singapore's Chinese food stalls and restaurants.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010084) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved source (flat rice noodles, boiled, Atkinson et al. 2021) and documented as an analogous estimate.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this prepared dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010084 as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB database entry for this dish. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as confirmed by the HPB source — this is a plant-based noodle dish with no animal-derived cholesterol-containing ingredients in the standard preparation. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate based on flat boiled rice noodles from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this specific Northwest-style preparation exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving (300 g): GL = (61 × 75.57) / 100 = 46.1, reported as 46.1 per 300 g serving. Note: the gi_source notes field contains the per-serving GL calculation detail. The dish is classified as vegetarian and vegan under standard preparation; individual vendor preparations may include egg or meat additions. Halal status is assumed based on standard plant-based ingredients but individual vendor compliance should be verified.
